data.content:
### Specify Package Manager with vercel.json installCommand

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/package-managers

This snippet demonstrates how to configure a specific package manager, such as pnpm, for a Vercel deployment using the `installCommand` property within the `vercel.json` file. This setting overrides the default package manager used by Vercel for that particular deployment.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "installCommand": "pnpm install"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Build Command in vercel.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/configure-a-build

Override the build command for a specific deployment by adding the buildCommand property to vercel.json. This allows custom build logic for individual deployments without affecting project-wide settings.

```json
{
  "buildCommand": "npm run build"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Override Install Command in Vercel

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/configuration

Configure a custom install command in vercel.json to override the default install command in Project Settings. This is useful for trying out different package managers. An empty string value will skip the install command entirely.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "installCommand": "npm install"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Redirect Object Definition

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/project-configuration

Defines the structure and properties of a single redirect rule used in Vercel configurations within `vercel.json`.

```APIDOC
## Redirect Object Definition

### Description
This object defines a single redirect rule, specifying how incoming paths should be mapped to new destinations, with options for permanence and conditional matching.

### Properties
- **source** (string) - Required - A pattern that matches each incoming pathname (excluding querystring).
- **destination** (string) - Required - A location destination defined as an absolute pathname or external URL.
- **permanent** (boolean) - Optional - Toggles between permanent (308, default `true`) and temporary (307) redirect.
- **statusCode** (integer) - Optional - Defines a custom status code for the redirect. Cannot be used with `permanent`.
- **has** (array) - Optional - An array of `has` objects for conditional redirects based on the presence of specified properties.
- **missing** (array) - Optional - An array of `missing` objects for conditional redirects based on the absence of specified properties.
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel CLI globally

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/custom-environments

These commands install the Vercel Command Line Interface globally on your system using different package managers. The Vercel CLI is essential for local development, linking projects, and managing deployments.

```npm
npm i -g vercel
```

```bun
bun i -g vercel
```

```yarn
yarn global add vercel
```

```pnpm
pnpm i -g vercel
```

--------------------------------

### Vercel Project Redirects Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/project-configuration

Illustrates how to configure individual redirect rules within the `vercel.json` file for a Vercel project, including conditional redirects.

```APIDOC
## Vercel Project Redirects Configuration

### Description
This configuration snippet demonstrates how to define an array of `redirect` objects in your `vercel.json` file to manage routing for your project. This includes basic and conditional redirects.

### Configuration Example
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "redirects": [
    {
      "source": "/start",
      "destination": "/end",
      "has": [
        {
          "type": "header",
          "key": "X-Custom-Header",
          "value": {
            "pre": "valid",
            "suf": "value"
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "source": "/old-page",
      "destination": "/new-page",
      "permanent": true
    }
  ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Define Vercel URL Redirects in vercel.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/project-configuration

This property allows defining custom URL redirects for a Vercel project. Redirects can be temporary (307), permanent (308, 301), and support various matching patterns including exact paths, wildcards, regex, and conditional headers. Redirects are configured as an array of objects within `vercel.json`.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "redirects": [
    { "source": "/me", "destination": "/profile.html", "permanent": false }
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "redirects": [
    { "source": "/me", "destination": "/profile.html", "permanent": true }
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "redirects": [
    { "source": "/user", "destination": "/api/user", "statusCode": 301 }
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "redirects": [
    {
      "source": "/view-source",
      "destination": "https://github.com/vercel/vercel"
    }
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "redirects": [
    {
      "source": "/(.*)",
      "destination": "https://vercel.com/docs"
    }
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "redirects": [
    {
      "source": "/blog/:path*",
      "destination": "/news/:path*"
    }
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "redirects": [
    {
      "source": "/post/:path(\\d{1,})",
      "destination": "/news/:path*"
    }
  ]
}
```

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "redirects": [
    {
      "source": "/:path((?!uk/).*)",
      "has": [
        {
          "type": "header",
          "key": "x-vercel-ip-country",
          "value": "GB"
        }
      ],
      "destination": "/uk/:path*",
      "permanent": false
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Define vercel-build script in package.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Configure a custom build script in package.json that runs before Vercel Function deployment. The nearest package.json to the function is used for both installing dependencies and building. This allows preprocessing or generating files needed by the function.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "vercel-build": "node ./build.js"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Schema Autocomplete Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/configuration

Configure your vercel.json file with schema validation to enable autocompletion, type checking, and validation in your editor. Add the $schema property at the top of your vercel.json file to get these benefits.

```APIDOC
## Schema Autocomplete Configuration

### Description
Adds JSON schema validation to vercel.json for autocompletion and type checking support in editors.

### Property
`$schema` (string)

### Type
string

### Value
`https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json`

### Usage Example
```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json"
}
```

### Benefits
- Enables autocompletion in supported editors
- Provides type checking for configuration properties
- Validates schema against Vercel's specification
```

--------------------------------

### Configure vercel-build Script in package.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/functions/runtimes/node-js/advanced-node-configuration

Define a custom build script in package.json that executes during Vercel Function deployment. The nearest package.json to the function is used for both installing dependencies and running the build script.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "vercel-build": "node ./build.js"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Add Schema Autocomplete to vercel.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/configuration

Configure schema autocomplete, type checking, and validation for vercel.json by adding a $schema property referencing the Vercel OpenAPI schema. This enables IDE support for configuration properties.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel CLI with npm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/cms/agility-cms

Install the Vercel CLI using the npm package manager. This tool is required to pull environment variables from Vercel to your local project during development and deployment setup.

```bash
npm i vercel
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Output Directory in vercel.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/configure-a-build

Override the output directory for a specific deployment by adding the outputDirectory property to vercel.json. This specifies which directory contains the built files to be served statically by Vercel.

```json
{
  "outputDirectory": "dist"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure devCommand in vercel.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Adds the `devCommand` property to the Vercel configuration file to override the default development command. This allows customization of how `vercel dev` behaves locally when developing your project.

```json
{
  "devCommand": "next dev --port $PORT"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Framework Override in vercel.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/configure-a-build

Override the detected framework for a specific deployment by adding the framework property to vercel.json. This allows you to specify a different framework than what Vercel auto-detects for individual deployments.

```json
{
  "framework": "next"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel CLI using Package Managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/analytics/quickstart

Install the Vercel CLI tool as a prerequisite for Vercel project management. Supports multiple package managers including npm, yarn, pnpm, and bun. Choose the command appropriate for your project's package manager.

```bash
npm i vercel
```

```bash
yarn i vercel
```

```bash
pnpm i vercel
```

```bash
bun i vercel
```

--------------------------------

### Configure BotID rewrites and headers in vercel.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/botid/get-started

Set up BotID proxy rewrites and security headers in vercel.json for frameworks other than Next.js and Nuxt. This configuration applies only in production and routes bot protection requests to Vercel's API.

```json
{
  "rewrites": [
    {
      "source": "/149e9513-01fa-4fb0-aad4-566afd725d1b/2d206a39-8ed7-437e-a3be-862e0f06eea3/a-4-a/c.js",
      "destination": "https://api.vercel.com/bot-protection/v1/challenge"
    },
    {
      "source": "/149e9513-01fa-4fb0-aad4-566afd725d1b/2d206a39-8ed7-437e-a3be-862e0f06eea3/:path*",
      "destination": "https://api.vercel.com/bot-protection/v1/proxy/:path*"
    }
  ],
  "headers": [
    {
      "source": "/149e9513-01fa-4fb0-aad4-566afd725d1b/2d206a39-8ed7-437e-a3be-862e0f06eea3/:path*",
      "headers": [
        {
          "key": "X-Frame-Options",
          "value": "SAMEORIGIN"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Configure build script in package.json for Vercel

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/errors/error-list

Define a build script in package.json that performs a static build and outputs to a public directory. This configuration is required for Vercel deployments when no api directory or vercel.json file exists. The build script should specify the framework build command with the output directory set to 'public' at the project root.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "build": "[my-framework] build --output public"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Trailing Slash Configuration

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/project-configuration

Controls URL trailing slash behavior for all paths in a Vercel deployment. Configure whether paths should automatically redirect with or without trailing slashes using the trailingSlash property in vercel.json.

```APIDOC
## trailingSlash Configuration

### Description
Configures automatic redirect behavior for trailing slashes on URL paths.

### Type
Boolean or undefined

### Default Value
undefined

---

## trailingSlash: false

### Behavior
When set to false, visiting a path ending with a forward slash responds with HTTP 308 status code and redirects to the path without the trailing slash.

### Example Redirect
- Request: `/about/`
- Redirects to: `/about`

### Configuration

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "trailingSlash": false
}
```

---

## trailingSlash: true

### Behavior
When set to true, visiting a path without a trailing slash responds with HTTP 308 status code and redirects to the path with a trailing slash.

### Example Redirects
- Request: `/about` → Redirects to: `/about/`
- Request: `/about/styles` → Redirects to: `/about/styles/`
- Request: `/about/styles.css` → NO redirect (file extensions excluded)

### Configuration

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "trailingSlash": true
}
```

---

## trailingSlash: undefined

### Behavior
When undefined, visiting paths with or without trailing slashes serves the same content without redirecting.

### Example Behavior
- Request: `/about` → Serves content (no redirect)
- Request: `/about/` → Serves same content (no redirect)

### Configuration

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "trailingSlash": undefined
}
```

### Note
This configuration is not recommended as it may cause search engines to index duplicate content at different URLs
```

--------------------------------

### Configure bulkRedirectsPath in vercel.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/redirects/bulk-redirects/getting-started

Add the bulkRedirectsPath property to your vercel.json configuration file, pointing to your redirect file location. This can reference a single file or a folder containing multiple redirect files.

```json
{
  "bulkRedirectsPath": "redirects.csv"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel SDK using pnpm

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/sdk

This command installs the `@vercel/sdk` package into your project's dependencies using pnpm. It is the first step to enable access to Vercel's REST API resources within your application.

```bash
pnpm i @vercel/sdk
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vercel Sandbox CLI with package managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/vercel-sandbox/cli-reference

Installs the Vercel Sandbox command-line interface globally using pnpm, yarn, npm, or bun. This setup allows you to manage sandboxes from your terminal across your system.

```bash
pnpm i sandbox
```

```bash
yarn i sandbox
```

```bash
npm i sandbox
```

```bash
bun i sandbox
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Vercel Conformance via CLI

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Use this command to automatically set up Vercel Conformance in your repository. It installs the necessary packages and configures initial settings, allowing you to gradually resolve existing Conformance issues.

```shell
pnpm --package=@vercel-private/conformance dlx vercel-conformance init
```

```shell
yarn -DW add @vercel-private/conformance && yarn vercel-conformance init
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Vercel Functions with vercel.json for Other Frameworks

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/functions/configuring-functions/advanced-configuration

Configure individual Vercel Functions with memory and maxDuration settings in vercel.json for non-Next.js frameworks. Routes are placed in the /api directory and support both .js and .ts file extensions for flexible bundling.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "functions": {
    "api/hello.js": {
      "memory": 3009,
      "maxDuration": 60
    },
    "api/another.js": {
      "memory": 1024,
      "maxDuration": 30
    }
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "functions": {
    "api/hello.ts": {
      "memory": 3009,
      "maxDuration": 60
    },
    "api/another.ts": {
      "memory": 1024,
      "maxDuration": 30
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Local Configuration File for Vercel CLI

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/llms-full

Sets the path to a specific `vercel.json` file for local project configuration. This option, also available as shorthand `-A`, allows overriding default or global settings with a custom local config.

```terminal
vercel --local-config /path-to/vercel.json
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Vercel Functions with vercel.json for Next.js

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/functions/configuring-functions/advanced-configuration

Configure individual Vercel Functions with memory and maxDuration settings in vercel.json for Next.js projects. This allows separate bundling of routes with different configurations. Supports both .js and .ts file extensions.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "functions": {
    "pages/api/hello.js": {
      "memory": 3009,
      "maxDuration": 60
    },
    "pages/api/another.js": {
      "memory": 1024,
      "maxDuration": 30
    }
  }
}
```

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "functions": {
    "pages/api/hello.ts": {
      "memory": 3009,
      "maxDuration": 60
    },
    "pages/api/another.ts": {
      "memory": 1024,
      "maxDuration": 30
    }
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Rewrite Configuration in vercel.json

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/rewrites

Defines the fundamental structure for setting up rewrites in a Vercel project using the vercel.json configuration file. This basic format applies to both same-application and external rewrites, requiring a source path pattern and destination path or URL.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://openapi.vercel.sh/vercel.json",
  "rewrites": [
    {
      "source": "/source-path",
      "destination": "/destination-path"
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install LaunchDarkly Vercel Server SDK across package managers

Source: https://vercel.com/docs/edge-config/integrations/launchdarkly-edge-config

Commands to install LaunchDarkly's Vercel Server SDK using pnpm, yarn, npm, and bun. Required for implementing feature flag functionality in Vercel middleware.

```shell
pnpm i @launchdarkly/vercel-server-sdk
```

```shell
yarn add @launchdarkly/vercel-server-sdk
```

```shell
npm i @launchdarkly/vercel-server-sdk
```

```shell
bun add @launchdarkly/vercel-server-sdk
```
